events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need diagnose most likely fault from evidence: ball joint play (video item - pry test). In-service asset. Suspension & steering. Ball joint play indicates worn/degraded/failed ball joint. Risk high/critical? Loose ball joint affects steering control, tire wear, safety. Decision fail because non-compliant/dangerous? The task: diagnose most likely fault. Since observed field condition is ball joint play (pry test), likely ball joint wear/excessive looseness. Need fill schema. decision: fail (dangerous/non-compliant). risk: high or critical? Ball joint failure can cause loss of control; critical. work_stage: in-service. component_conditions: degraded, failed, worn? Schema allowed list includes installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. We can include worn, degraded, failed, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 100? The diagnosis task? Could be in-service inspection, observed condition. value: maybe amount of play? Not given numeric. Use 0. sound_source not asked. confidence high 0.9. event_time 0. rate 0. order empty. workable empty. findings: excessive ball joint play detected via pry test; joint is worn beyond service limit; loss of steering/suspension integrity. actions: replace ball joint, inspect related components, perform alignment per OEM specs, verify torque. rationale: pry test showing play indicates worn ball joint; in-service steering/suspension ball joint with free movement fails OEM specs and is safety-critical. references: OEM service info, alignment specs. Need ensure valid JSON. No prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",

  "value": 0,

  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Excessive ball joint play confirmed by pry test",
    "Ball joint is worn beyond OEM service limit",
    "Steering and suspension integrity compromised"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace the failed ball joint with OEM-specified component",
    "Inspect related suspension and steering linkage for secondary damage",
    "Perform wheel alignment per OEM alignment specs after repair",
    "Torque all fasteners to specification and re-test for play"
  ],
  "rationale": "A ball joint that exhibits free play during a pry test has lost its preload and bearing surface integrity, indicating a worn/degraded/failed joint. Because ball joints maintain steering knuckle and control arm alignment, excessive play is a safety-critical defect that violates OEM service limits and alignment requirements.",
  "references": [
    "OEM service information",
    "OEM alignment specifications"
  ]
}
